briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 25, 2026. Today's main topics include the record-breaking heatwave sweeping across Europe, difficulties in climate finance negotiations, domestic and international efforts in climate change response and adaptation, various initiatives for achieving carbon neutrality, and trends in the revitalization of the renewable energy market.

sections.title: 🔴 Record Heatwave Across Europe and Climate Finance Difficulties
sections.content: Europe is once again suffering from a record-breaking heatwave, with the rate of warming in Europe proceeding twice as fast as the global average [6, 31]. This has led to severe casualties, including 212 deaths in Spain alone and 48 in France, billions of euros in losses to the German economy, and a more than doubling of dangerous humid heat days worldwide [20, 113, 117, 127, 137]. Amidst this climate crisis, pre-negotiations held ahead of the 31st Conference of the Parties to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (COP31) failed to reach an agreement on key agendas, including climate finance, concluding 'empty-handed' and raising concerns for future meetings [114, 124].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Active Domestic and International Climate Change Response and Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: Various efforts for climate change response and adaptation are actively underway both domestically and internationally. In Korea, the Rural Development Administration has secured breeding technology to respond to climate change by developing pre-harvest sprouting resistant rapeseed [1, 16], and the Korean Society of Climate Change Research held an academic conference to explore new standards for climate adaptation, disaster, and infrastructure [2, 9]. Furthermore, domestic research institutions, including the Korea Polar Research Institute, are strengthening joint research on polar climate change with the Danish Meteorological Institute [3, 8, 12]. Technological advancements are also evident, such as the development of AI-based wildfire risk prediction technology [7] and the expansion of climate crisis adaptation information platforms [22, 29]. The Greenhouse Gas Inventory & Research Center and the Korean Society of Climate Change Research are cooperating to respond to changes in international climate statistical standards [10, 14, 15, 23, 25], and globally, Olympic athletes are urging solutions to climate change [118, 138, 141], demonstrating a global response.

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality, Beyond Regulation to Growth Strategy: Accelerated Domestic Implementation
sections.content: In Korea, there is an accelerating movement to transform carbon neutrality from a regulation into a new growth strategy. The Federation of Korean Industries (FKI) and Seoul National University have advocated for carbon neutrality as a growth strategy, proposing five key K-GX tasks [5, 32, 45, 63, 74, 91]. Local governments and public institutions are encouraging citizen participation through carbon neutrality point systems and energy-saving campaigns [34, 35, 36, 38, 49, 50, 51, 55, 62, 65, 66, 77, 83, 89, 90, 93]. Korea Electric Power Corporation and the National Police Agency have signed a business agreement to streamline security facility management for energy saving and carbon neutrality implementation [41, 42, 43, 44, 48, 54, 61, 69, 75, 79, 84]. Furthermore, Gyeongsangbuk-do Province and Pohang City are actively fostering the climate tech industry to secure core carbon neutrality technologies [40, 47, 56, 80, 81]. At the Korea Land, Infrastructure and Transport Technology Fair, the demonstration results of a 'Carbon Spatial Map' that visualizes urban greenhouse gases were unveiled, expected to contribute to efficient carbon management [52, 78].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Market Activation and Investment Expansion
sections.content: The renewable energy sector is establishing itself as a key driver for climate change response through expanded investment and market activation worldwide. In the domestic stock market, renewable energy-related stocks, including SK E&S and Paru, are showing strong performance and hitting daily limits, attracting high investor interest [21, 96, 97, 102, 103, 104, 107, 109, 110, 111]. The National Growth Fund is investing hundreds of billions of won in the Yeongyang onshore wind power project and renewable energy infrastructure construction, supporting the expansion of renewable energy [99, 100]. China is also continuing policy support, such as strengthening renewable energy consumption obligations starting in August [98]. Additionally, the Ministry of Climate is promoting the mandatory installation of 'grid-forming' facilities to prevent blackouts caused by renewable energy, striving to build a stable power supply system [105]. These efforts are expected to drive continuous progress in the domestic and international renewable energy transition, similar to the sustained growth of renewable energy in the United States despite federal policy uncertainties [145].

sections.title: ⚠️ Complex Threats of Climate Change: Pest Spread and Societal Impacts
sections.content: Climate change poses complex threats across natural ecosystems and society as a whole. Domestically, pine wilt disease has spread nationwide due to climate change and anthropogenic causes, affecting 1.77 million pine trees [4]. Warnings have also been issued about the need for thorough preparation for rice pest and disease control due to rapid climate change [11, 18]. Internationally, Texas's rejection of climate change plans has created a crisis in the Corpus Christi area [120], and the pattern of natural disasters is changing, with a surge in lightning-induced wildfires in Washington [123]. Furthermore, climate change is affecting the transmission patterns of dengue fever [134], and rising sea levels are causing significant geopolitical issues that redefine the meaning of statehood [132]. Amidst these complex threats, changes are also appearing in citizens' daily lives, such as an increased demand for umbrellas and parasols in preparation for heatwaves and torrential rains caused by climate change [115].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights both the urgent threats posed by climate change and the diverse domestic and international efforts coexisting to overcome them. In particular, the shift towards transforming carbon neutrality into a growth strategy and expanding renewable energy investments is positive. However, sustained attention and swift action are required regarding the difficulties in climate finance negotiations and the complex adverse impacts on ecosystems. This concludes today's climate briefing.
